name: River Pisuerga bank – circular from Tordesillas via Robladillo (EV1)
name_original: Orilla del río Pisuerga – circular desde Tordesillas por Robladillo (EV1)
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Tordesillas traverse a landscape characterized by the confluence of the Duero and Pisuerga rivers, offering extensive riverine paths. The region features a mix of open cereal steppes, significant pine and holm oak forests, and riparian woodlands along the riverbanks. Gentle gradients are common, with some routes incorporating modest elevation changes, providing diverse terrain for cyclists. This area combines natural beauty with historical landmarks, making it suitable for exploring by bike. Best touring cycling routes around Tordesillas The most popular touring cycling route is River Pisuerga bank – circular from Tordesillas via Robladillo (EV1), a 27.0 miles (43.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route follows the banks of the Pisuerga River, offering scenic views and connecting to the EuroVelo 1 route. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Historic Center of Tordesillas – Royal Convent of Santa Clara loop from Tordesillas, an easy 12.2 miles (19.7 km) path. This route explores the town's historic center and passes by the Royal Convent of Santa Clara. Local touring cyclists also love the Tordesillas Historic Center – Historic Center of Tordesillas loop from Tordesillas, a 21.6 miles (34.7 km) trail leading through the historic core and surrounding areas, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Tordesillas is defined by river valleys, cereal steppes, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging excursions. The routes in Tordesillas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tordesillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Tordesillas?
A: Tordesillas offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 70 options available. These routes cater to different preferences, from short, easy loops to longer, more challenging excursions.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Tordesillas?
A: The region around Tordesillas features diverse landscapes. You'll find scenic routes along the banks of the Duero and Pisuerga rivers, extensive cereal steppes, and significant pine and holm oak forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175321922/river-pisuerga-bank-circular-from-tordesillas-via-robladillo-ev1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;River Pisuerga bank – circular from Tordesillas via Robladillo (EV1)&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, highlights the beautiful riparian areas.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Tordesillas has several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14881303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Tordesillas – Royal Convent of Santa Clara loop from Tordesillas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 19.7 km path that explores the town's historic center. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tordesillas Historic Center loop from Tordesillas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16.8 km long.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes integrate Tordesillas' rich history. You can cycle through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Tordesillas&lt;/a&gt;, pass by the Royal Convent of Santa Clara, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2814203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Simancas Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14881303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Tordesillas – Royal Convent of Santa Clara loop from Tordesillas&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for combining cycling with cultural stops.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Simancas Stone Bridge – Historic Center of Tordesillas loop from Tordesillas&lt;/a&gt; offer a more demanding experience. This route is over 100 km long and features significant elevation changes, providing a rewarding ride through diverse terrain.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Tordesillas?
A: The touring cycling routes in Tordesillas are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to pine forests, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Tordesillas?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Tordesillas are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175321922/river-pisuerga-bank-circular-from-tordesillas-via-robladillo-ev1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;River Pisuerga bank – circular from Tordesillas via Robladillo (EV1)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a convenient loop for exploring the riverbanks.

Q: Can I find routes that connect to longer cycling networks like EuroVelo?
A: Tordesillas is part of broader cycling networks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175321922/river-pisuerga-bank-circular-from-tordesillas-via-robladillo-ev1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;River Pisuerga bank – circular from Tordesillas via Robladillo (EV1)&lt;/a&gt; route connects to the EuroVelo 1, making it a potential stop or starting point for longer journeys through the Ribera del Duero region.

Q: What natural reserves or unique natural features can be explored by bike?
A: The region is home to the Riberas de Castronuño y Vega del Duero Natural Reserve, a significant natural space known for its diverse water birds and riparian forests. Cycling routes often venture through farmlands towards this reserve, offering unique beauty and opportunities for birdwatching. You can also find routes traversing stone pine forests and holm oak dehesas.

Q: Is there public transport available to access the cycling routes in Tordesillas?
A: Tordesillas is a well-connected town, and while specific public transport details for route access aren't provided, its position as a point on longer cycling journeys suggests accessibility. It's advisable to check local bus or train schedules for connections to Tordesillas if you plan to arrive without a vehicle.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Tordesillas?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather than summer or winter. These seasons provide comfortable conditions for exploring the diverse landscapes and historical sites.

Q: Are there any specific routes that highlight the Duero River?
A: The majestic Duero River is a prominent feature of the Tordesillas landscape. Many routes offer impressive views from the town's medieval bridge and follow the river's course. The 'Ribera del Duero' area, in particular, offers routes that combine nature with opportunities to explore the renowned wine regions along the river.
